skill.name: imagen
skill.description: Guides image generation with Google Gemini for frontend assets, documentation visuals, diagrams, icons, and concept art.
skill.summary: Use Gemini image generation prompts for project visuals and UI assets.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution and weak-crypto findings are false positives from Markdown examples and normal words in SKILL.md. The skill still carries medium risk because it documents a workflow that uses an external Gemini API call, requires GEMINI_API_KEY, and references a Python helper script that is not present in the scanned file tree.

medium_findings.title: External API and Credential Dependent Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill describes calling the Google Gemini API for image generation and requires GEMINI_API_KEY. This is an intended use case, but it requires users to provide a secret to a workflow that depends on external network access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly states that it calls the Google Gemini API and requires GEMINI_API_KEY. No malicious exfiltration is shown, so the concern is operational and credential-handling risk rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Referenced Helper Script Is Not Included
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run scripts/generate_image.py, but the scanned file structure contains only SKILL.md. This makes the executable behavior unverifiable in this package and may cause users to supply their own unreviewed script.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples reference a Python script, and the report file structure lists only SKILL.md. The missing script is a clear packaging and reviewability issue, but not direct evidence of malicious code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backticks and fenced examples as Ruby or shell execution. These lines are documentation examples, not executable code in the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown prose or fenced usage examples. There is no executable Ruby or shell source file in the scanned file structure.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Source Metadata
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL flagged by static analysis is the skill source link in frontmatter. It is not an executable network request or data transfer path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a source metadata URL. I found no code at that line and no evidence that this URL receives user data or credentials.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm hits, but the cited lines describe image generation metadata or examples. No cryptographic operation is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain description text, workflow steps, or example output text. I found no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Teams often need quick visuals while building interfaces, docs, and prototypes. This skill provides Gemini image generation guidance for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Describes when to use Gemini image generation during project work.
Provides command examples for prompts, output paths, and image size.
Documents GEMINI_API_KEY as the required credential.
Explains that generated images are saved as PNG files.
Shows example workflows for frontend visuals, documentation, and UI assets.
limitations: The referenced generate_image.py script is not included in the scanned package.
Image generation requires external Google Gemini API access and a valid key.
The skill does not document rate limits, costs, or content policy handling.

use_cases.title: Create Landing Page Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate hero images, abstract backgrounds, and product visuals for frontend prototypes.

use_cases.title: Add Documentation Illustrations
use_cases.description: Create diagrams and concept visuals for README files, architecture notes, and internal guides.

use_cases.title: Draft UI Asset Concepts
use_cases.description: Produce placeholder avatars, icons, and visual references before final design work.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one visual asset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a PNG image of a clean technology dashboard hero visual for a SaaS landing page.

prompt_templates.title: Set an Output Location
prompt_templates.scenario: Save an asset where a project can use it.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a minimalist music app icon and save it under the project assets folder.

prompt_templates.title: Create Documentation Art
prompt_templates.scenario: Produce a visual explanation for technical documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a clear diagram-style image that shows a microservices system with an API gateway, services, and database.

prompt_templates.title: Specify Asset Constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with style, format, and use constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a high-resolution PNG hero image for a fintech dashboard with calm colors, no text, and enough negative space for overlay copy.

output_examples.input: Create a hero image for a cloud analytics landing page.
output_examples.output: A PNG image is generated and saved to the requested project location for use in the page layout.

output_examples.input: Make a diagram for a README that explains a microservices architecture.
output_examples.output: The skill guides creation of a documentation-ready visual that can be placed in the README.

output_examples.input: Generate placeholder avatars for a user profile component.
output_examples.output: The workflow produces PNG avatar assets suitable for frontend prototyping.

best_practices: Use specific prompts that include style, subject, size, and intended placement.
Store generated files in a clear project asset folder with descriptive names.
Protect GEMINI_API_KEY and avoid printing it in logs, prompts, or screenshots.
anti_patterns: Do not assume the referenced Python helper exists without checking the project.
Do not use vague prompts when the image must match a product or brand.
Do not commit generated secrets, API keys, or private prompt inputs.

faq.question: Does this skill include an image generation script?
faq.answer: No. The scanned package contains SKILL.md only, although it references scripts/generate_image.py.

faq.question: Which image model does the skill mention?
faq.answer: It mentions gemini-3-pro-image-preview for Google Gemini image generation.

faq.question: What credential is required?
faq.answer: The skill requires GEMINI_API_KEY to be set as an environment variable.

faq.question: What file format does it describe?
faq.answer: The documentation says generated images are saved as PNG files.

faq.question: Can it help with frontend development?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes workflows for hero images, icons, placeholders, and other UI assets.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It can be published with a warning because it depends on external API access and a missing helper script.
